seoTitle: South Bunbury vs South Carnarvon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Bunbury and South Carnarvon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Bunbury vs South Carnarvon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Bunbury and South Carnarvon.
heading: South Bunbury vs South Carnarvon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Bunbury, Western Australia and South Carnarvon, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Carnarvon has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to South Bunbury (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Bunbury or South Carnarvon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Bunbury has a flood risk score of 43/100, while South Carnarvon scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Bunbury scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. South Carnarvon scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Bunbury or South Carnarvon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Carnarvon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
